basic_details.job_title: NCL Recruitment 2026: Mining Sirdar & Surveyor Posts, Apply Online from 14 July
basic_details.total_vacancies: Mining Sirdar T&S Gr. C, Surveyor (Mining) T&S Gr. B
basic_details.short_information: Northern Coalfields Limited (NCL), a Miniratna subsidiary of Coal India Limited, has released an Employment Notification (Ref. NCL/SING/HR/Direct-Recruitment/2026-27/246) dated 10.07.2026 inviting online applications from Indian nationals for the statutory mining posts of Mining Sirdar T&S Gr. C and Surveyor (Mining) T&S Gr. B. These posts are for regular deployment across NCL's Mines/Establishments in Singrauli District (Madhya Pradesh) and Sonebhadra District (Uttar Pradesh). Online registration opens on 14-07-2026 and closes on 05-08-2026.

important_dates.computer based test (cbt) date: Not yet released - will be intimated through notice and Admit Card

application_fee.general_fee: ₹1000.00/- plus applicable GST ₹180/-, totaling ₹1180/- (Rupees One Thousand One Hundred Eighty Only)
application_fee.details: Applications without the prescribed fee will not be entertained and will be summarily rejected; no representation against such rejection will be entertained. Fee once paid will neither be refunded nor adjusted under any circumstances. Any tax or bank charges other than the application fee will be borne by the candidate. Fee is payable through online mode only.

age_limit.details: Candidates must have attained a minimum age of 18 years as on the Crucial Date (05-08-2026), and age must not exceed the upper age limit of 30 years on the Crucial Date, subject to relaxations below. | The Date of Birth recorded in the Matriculation/Secondary Examination Certificate, or marksheet of Matriculation/10th Standard or equivalent certificate issued by a Central/State Board, will be considered valid.

qualification.details: Option 1: | Matriculate or equivalent examination from any recognized Board of Examination. Valid Mining Sirdarship Certificate of Competency issued by DGMS under Coal Mines Regulation 2017, or any other certificate in Mining entitling the applicant to work as Mining Sirdar as per Coal Mines Regulation 2017. Valid Gas Testing Certificate. Valid First Aid Certificate. | Option 2: | Matriculate or equivalent examination from any recognized Board of Examination. Degree or Diploma in Mining Engineering from any recognized Institute. Valid Overman Competency Certificate issued by DGMS under Coal Mines Regulation 2017, or any other certificate in Mining entitling the applicant to work as Mining Sirdar as per Coal Mines Regulation 2017. Valid Gas Testing Certificate. Valid First Aid Certificate. | Option 1: | Matriculate or equivalent examination from any recognized Board of Examination. Valid Surveyors' Certificate of Competency (SCC) granted under Coal Mines Regulation 2017, or any other certificate in Mining entitling the applicant to work as Surveyor in Mines as per Coal Mines Regulation 2017. | Option 2: | Degree or Diploma in Mining/Mine Surveying Engineering from any recognized Institute. Valid Surveyors' Certificate of Competency (SCC) granted under Coal Mines Regulation 2017, or any other certificate in Mining entitling the applicant to work as Surveyor in Mines as per Coal Mines Regulation 2017. | The prescribed minimum/essential qualifications are the minimum requirement; candidates possessing higher and relevant qualifications may also apply. Qualifications acquired through distance learning/part-time study or their equivalent will not be considered.

salary_details.post: Entry Level Grade & Pay Scale (As per NCWA-XI)
salary_details.mining sirdar, t&s grade c: T&S Gr. C (Monthly Rated); Basic pay ₹47,330.25 per month
salary_details.surveyor, t&s grade b: T&S Gr. B (Monthly Rated); Basic pay ₹51,103.12 per month
salary_details.details: Besides basic pay, the post carries other allowances such as DA, attendance bonus, special allowance, transport subsidy, and medical facility as per the Company's rules/National Coal Wage Agreement.

selection_process: Post Maximum Marks Category Minimum Cut-Off Marks Required
Mining Sirdar T&S Gr. C 100 UR, EWS 50 marks
SC/ST/ESM/OBC-NCL 40 marks
Surveyor T&S Gr. B (Mining) 100 UR, EWS 50 marks

Eligible candidates meeting minimum eligibility criteria as on the Crucial Date must appear for a Computer Based Test (CBT). Selection is based on relative CBT score. Candidates are allowed to appear in the CBT solely on the basis of their declaration of eligibility in the online application form. Scrutiny/verification of eligibility claims is done only after CBT results are declared
and only for candidates who clear the cut-off marks. CBT is conducted separately for each post
carries 100 marks
and is of 90 minutes duration in one sitting. Section A: Technical knowledge (discipline-related)
70 MCQs. Section B: General Knowledge
General Awareness
Reasoning
Verbal & Mental Ability
and Quantitative Aptitude
30 MCQs. Each question carries 1 mark; there is no negative marking for wrong answers
and no marks for unattempted questions. The question paper is bilingual (English and Hindi); in case of ambiguity
the English version is valid and final. The CBT will be held at Test Cities as mentioned in the online application form; candidates may opt for their preferred city
which cannot be changed after submission.
Candidates who score equal to or above the cut-off will be notified for scrutiny/verification of testimonials
in order of merit position
up to the extent positions remain unfilled. In case of a tie in CBT marks
resolution follows this order: (1) higher score in Section A
(2) older Date of Birth preferred
(3) alphabetical order of name as per matriculation certificate.

syllabus.details: Opencast coal mine working; bench formation, dump management, and haul road design Shot firing and transport and use of explosives in mines Safety issues in opencast workings, transport of material, working at heights, marching and deployment of heavy machines Preliminary idea about reclamation operation in opencast mining Preliminary understanding of safety management plan Provisions of the Coal Mines Regulations 2017, Rules and Bye-laws under the Mines Act 1952, relating to safety of persons employed in mines and duties of Sirdars and Shot firers Mines Rules 1955, Mines Vocational Training Rules 1966, Mines Rescue Rules 1985, DGMS Circulars Writing of reports | Linear measurement, chain surveying, optical square EDM: principles, types, correction, and selection of instruments Angular measurement: prismatic compass, bearing of lines, local attraction, magnetic declination Plan table surveying and contouring Miners' dials and other compass instruments Theodolite, traversing, and traverse calculations Total stations: surveying, errors, adjustments, applications Levelling: instruments, types, geometrical, trigonometric and physical levelling, contouring Tachometry Controlled surveys: triangulation, trilateration, GPS and total station applications Field astronomy, Gyro Theodolite, azimuth determination National grid, map projections, geodesy Photogrammetry and remote sensing in mining Theory of errors and adjustments Area and volume calculation, earthwork estimation Borehole surveying, dip, strike, outcrop and fault problems Mine plans, legislation, and duties of surveyors Geological map reading Computer applications in mine surveying, 3D laser profiling Dump/high wall stability monitoring Software knowledge: Surpac, AutoCAD, Liscad, Autoplotter, Riscan Pro Modern survey equipment: 3D Terrestrial Laser Scanner, GPS, Drone | General Knowledge - India and international relations, General Science General Awareness - Sports, Defense, Books, Prizes, Indian democracy Reasoning, Verbal & Mental Ability - Synonym & Antonym (Hindi/English), Grammar, Relationship Quantitative Aptitude - Work relationship, Profit & Loss, Speed

how_to_apply: Log in to the official NCL website using URL www.nclcil.in > Career > Recruitment > Employment Notification for Direct Recruitment of various Statutory posts > Apply Online.
Read the complete Employment Notification carefully (available in English and Hindi) to assess your eligibility.
Candidates other than SC/ST/Ex-Servicemen/Departmental Candidates must pay a non-refundable application fee of ₹1180/- through the online facility.
Upload the scanned photograph, signature, and all required documents as specified in the notification.
A candidate can apply for only a single post against this Employment Notification.
Verify all details (Date of Birth, Category, Contact Details, Qualifications) carefully before final submission, as no correction is possible after submission.
If more than one application is received from a candidate, the most recent application will be treated as final.
No hard copies of the application or testimonials need to be posted or dispatched to NCL.
important_instructions: A candidate can apply for only one post against this Employment Notification. All qualifications must be recognized by AICTE/UGC/appropriate Indian Statutory Authorities. Mere issuance of Admit Card does not imply that candidature has been finally cleared by NCL; candidature remains provisional until eligibility is verified against original documents after clearing the CBT. No relaxation in minimum educational qualification is admissible for any category of candidate. Where Grade (CGPA/OGPA/DGPA) is awarded, a conversion certificate from the concerned Board/University must be produced at the time of document verification. Candidates employed in Govt./Semi-Govt./PSU/Autonomous Bodies must produce a 'No Objection Certificate' at the time of document verification, if shortlisted, and must resign from their present job before joining NCL upon final selection. Selected candidates could be posted at any NCL Mine/Establishment and are liable to be transferred to other Coal India Limited subsidiaries during service. Appointment is subject to candidates being found medically fit as per CIL's Medical Examination Rules. SC/ST candidates called for CBT will be paid up to IInd Class Non-AC fare as per rules, subject to submitting the TA Claim format with required enclosures. No TA/DA will be paid for appearing in CBT, scrutiny/verification, or joining, except as provided in the notification. Court of Jurisdiction for any dispute will be the Honorable High Court of Jabalpur, M.P. In case of any ambiguity, the English version of the Employment Notification will be considered valid. Candidates must have an active email ID and mobile number valid for at least the next one year, as all correspondence will be through these or the NCL website.

content_sections.heading: NCL Recruitment 2026: Vacancy Details
content_sections.text: The vacancy breakup as per the source notification is given below. Note: for the Surveyor post, the category-wise figures in the source table do not fully reconcile arithmetically with the stated total, likely due to a formatting issue in the original PDF table - this has been reproduced exactly as given and should be verified against the official notification before relying on the category-wise breakup. UR: Unreserved, SC: Scheduled Caste, ST: Scheduled Tribe, OBC-NCL: Other Backward Class-Non Creamy Layer, T&S Gr.: Technical & Supervisory Grade, EWS: Economically Weaker Sections. Note: As per guidelines of the Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment, GoI, and CIL/C5A/MP&IR/RPwD/75 dated 27/09/2024, the posts of Mining Sirdar and Surveyor do not fall under the identified list of positions for Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBDs); hence reservation for PwBD candidates is not applicable for these posts. The total number of vacancies and reserved vacancies are provisional and may vary according to actual requirements.

content_sections.heading: NCL Recruitment 2026: Minimum Qualification Required
content_sections.text: Option 1: Matriculate or equivalent examination from any recognized Board of Examination. Valid Mining Sirdarship Certificate of Competency issued by DGMS under Coal Mines Regulation 2017, or any other certificate in Mining entitling the applicant to work as Mining Sirdar as per Coal Mines Regulation 2017. Valid Gas Testing Certificate. Valid First Aid Certificate. Option 2: Matriculate or equivalent examination from any recognized Board of Examination. Degree or Diploma in Mining Engineering from any recognized Institute. Valid Overman Competency Certificate issued by DGMS under Coal Mines Regulation 2017, or any other certificate in Mining entitling the applicant to work as Mining Sirdar as per Coal Mines Regulation 2017. Valid Gas Testing Certificate. Valid First Aid Certificate. Option 1: Matriculate or equivalent examination from any recognized Board of Examination. Valid Surveyors' Certificate of Competency (SCC) granted under Coal Mines Regulation 2017, or any other certificate in Mining entitling the applicant to work as Surveyor in Mines as per Coal Mines Regulation 2017. Option 2: Degree or Diploma in Mining/Mine Surveying Engineering from any recognized Institute. Valid Surveyors' Certificate of Competency (SCC) granted under Coal Mines Regulation 2017, or any other certificate in Mining entitling the applicant to work as Surveyor in Mines as per Coal Mines Regulation 2017. The prescribed minimum/essential qualifications are the minimum requirement; candidates possessing higher and relevant qualifications may also apply. Qualifications acquired through distance learning/part-time study or their equivalent will not be considered.

content_sections.heading: NCL Recruitment 2026: Age Limit
content_sections.text: Candidates must have attained a minimum age of 18 years as on the Crucial Date (05-08-2026), and age must not exceed the upper age limit of 30 years on the Crucial Date, subject to relaxations below. The Date of Birth recorded in the Matriculation/Secondary Examination Certificate, or marksheet of Matriculation/10th Standard or equivalent certificate issued by a Central/State Board, will be considered valid.

rows.maximum_age_relaxation_beyond_upper_age_: Actual period of service rendered in defense services + 3 years (SC/ST/OBC candidates also covered under ESM category are eligible for cumulative age relaxation under both categories, subject to actual age not exceeding 50 years)

rows.category: Departmental Candidates (all employees of CIL and its subsidiary companies)

content_sections.heading: NCL Recruitment 2026: Pay Scale and Probation Period
content_sections.text: Besides basic pay, the post carries other allowances such as DA, attendance bonus, special allowance, transport subsidy, and medical facility as per the Company's rules/National Coal Wage Agreement.

rows.entry_level_grade_pay_scale_as_per_ncwa_: T&S Gr. C (Monthly Rated); Basic pay ₹47,330.25 per month
T&S Gr. B (Monthly Rated); Basic pay ₹51,103.12 per month

content_sections.heading: NCL Recruitment 2026: Reservation
content_sections.text: Reservation of SC/ST/OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) Centre List/EWS/ESM will be applicable as per Government of India rules, guidelines, notifications, and amendments from time to time. The total number of vacancies and reserved vacancies are tentative and may vary according to actual requirements. SC/ST/OBC (NCL) candidates applying against Unreserved (UR) posts will not be eligible for relaxation in age and minimum qualifying marks, and will be treated at par with Unreserved candidates for selection (application fee exemption is still applicable for SC/ST candidates in such cases). Candidates seeking reservation must submit valid requisite certificates in the prescribed proforma issued by the competent authority. OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) Centre List candidates must ensure their caste/community certificate is valid and they do not fall in the creamy layer as on the Crucial Date. SC/ST/OBC (Non-Creamy Layer)/EWS candidates selected on their own merit without relaxed standards will not be adjusted against reserved vacancies; they will be accommodated against unreserved vacancies. Ex-servicemen who have already secured Government employment in Group C & D posts on regular basis after availing ESM reservation benefits are not eligible for ESM reservation and fee concession again, subject to conditions in OM No. 36034/1/2014-Estt.(Res) dated 14/08/2014. EWS candidates must submit a valid Income & Asset certificate issued by the competent authority as per OM No. 36039/1/2019-Estt(Res) dated 31.01.2019, based on gross annual income of Financial Year 2025-2026.

content_sections.heading: NCL Recruitment 2026: Application Fee
content_sections.text: Applications without the prescribed fee will not be entertained and will be summarily rejected; no representation against such rejection will be entertained. Fee once paid will neither be refunded nor adjusted under any circumstances. Any tax or bank charges other than the application fee will be borne by the candidate. Fee is payable through online mode only.

rows.fee: ₹1000.00/- plus applicable GST ₹180/-, totaling ₹1180/- (Rupees One Thousand One Hundred Eighty Only)

content_sections.heading: NCL Recruitment 2026: Selection Process and Exam Pattern
content_sections.text: Eligible candidates meeting minimum eligibility criteria as on the Crucial Date must appear for a Computer Based Test (CBT). Selection is based on relative CBT score. Candidates are allowed to appear in the CBT solely on the basis of their declaration of eligibility in the online application form. Scrutiny/verification of eligibility claims is done only after CBT results are declared, and only for candidates who clear the cut-off marks. CBT is conducted separately for each post, carries 100 marks, and is of 90 minutes duration in one sitting. Section A: Technical knowledge (discipline-related), 70 MCQs. Section B: General Knowledge, General Awareness, Reasoning, Verbal & Mental Ability, and Quantitative Aptitude, 30 MCQs. Each question carries 1 mark; there is no negative marking for wrong answers, and no marks for unattempted questions. The question paper is bilingual (English and Hindi); in case of ambiguity, the English version is valid and final. The CBT will be held at Test Cities as mentioned in the online application form; candidates may opt for their preferred city, which cannot be changed after submission. Candidates who score equal to or above the cut-off will be notified for scrutiny/verification of testimonials, in order of merit position, up to the extent positions remain unfilled. In case of a tie in CBT marks, resolution follows this order: (1) higher score in Section A, (2) older Date of Birth preferred, (3) alphabetical order of name as per matriculation certificate.

India and international relations, General Science General Awareness - Sports, Defense, Books, Prizes, Indian democracy Reasoning, Verbal & Mental Ability - Synonym & Antonym (Hindi/English), Grammar, Relationship Quantitative Aptitude - Work relationship, Profit & Loss, Speed

content_sections.heading: NCL Recruitment 2026: Documents Required
content_sections.text: Scanned copy of recent passport-size color photograph (not older than 3 weeks, jpg/jpeg format) Scanned copy of signature in black ink pen (jpg/jpeg format) Scanned copy of Matriculation/Secondary Board level certificate For Mining Sirdar T&S Gr. C: valid Mining Sirdar/Overman's Certificate of Competency issued by DGMS, valid Gas Testing Certificate, valid First Aid Certificate For Surveyor (Mining) T&S Gr. B: valid Surveyor's Certificate of Competency (SCC) For Mining Sirdar T&S Gr. C: scanned copy of Degree/Diploma in Mining Engineering (if applicable) For Surveyor T&S Gr. B: scanned copy of Degree/Diploma in Mining/Mine Surveying Engineering (if applicable) OBC (Non-Creamy Layer)/SC/ST candidates: self-attested valid caste certificate in prescribed format EWS candidates: self-attested valid Income & Asset certificate as per OM No. 36039/1/2019-Estt(Res) dated 31.01.2019, based on FY 2025-2026 gross annual income Self-attested certificate in prescribed format for J&K domicile (if applicable)

content_sections.heading: NCL Recruitment 2026: How to Apply
content_sections.text: Log in to the official NCL website using URL www.nclcil.in > Career > Recruitment > Employment Notification for Direct Recruitment of various Statutory posts > Apply Online. Read the complete Employment Notification carefully (available in English and Hindi) to assess your eligibility. Candidates other than SC/ST/Ex-Servicemen/Departmental Candidates must pay a non-refundable application fee of ₹1180/- through the online facility. Upload the scanned photograph, signature, and all required documents as specified in the notification. A candidate can apply for only a single post against this Employment Notification. Verify all details (Date of Birth, Category, Contact Details, Qualifications) carefully before final submission, as no correction is possible after submission. If more than one application is received from a candidate, the most recent application will be treated as final. No hard copies of the application or testimonials need to be posted or dispatched to NCL.

content_sections.heading: Important Instructions
content_sections.text: A candidate can apply for only one post against this Employment Notification. All qualifications must be recognized by AICTE/UGC/appropriate Indian Statutory Authorities. Mere issuance of Admit Card does not imply that candidature has been finally cleared by NCL; candidature remains provisional until eligibility is verified against original documents after clearing the CBT. No relaxation in minimum educational qualification is admissible for any category of candidate. Where Grade (CGPA/OGPA/DGPA) is awarded, a conversion certificate from the concerned Board/University must be produced at the time of document verification. Candidates employed in Govt./Semi-Govt./PSU/Autonomous Bodies must produce a 'No Objection Certificate' at the time of document verification, if shortlisted, and must resign from their present job before joining NCL upon final selection. Selected candidates could be posted at any NCL Mine/Establishment and are liable to be transferred to other Coal India Limited subsidiaries during service. Appointment is subject to candidates being found medically fit as per CIL's Medical Examination Rules. SC/ST candidates called for CBT will be paid up to IInd Class Non-AC fare as per rules, subject to submitting the TA Claim format with required enclosures. No TA/DA will be paid for appearing in CBT, scrutiny/verification, or joining, except as provided in the notification. Court of Jurisdiction for any dispute will be the Honorable High Court of Jabalpur, M.P. In case of any ambiguity, the English version of the Employment Notification will be considered valid. Candidates must have an active email ID and mobile number valid for at least the next one year, as all correspondence will be through these or the NCL website.

faq.question: Q1. How many vacancies are there in NCL Recruitment 2026 for Mining Sirdar and Surveyor?
faq.answer: As per the source notification, there are 254 vacancies for Mining Sirdar T&S Gr. C and 5 vacancies for Surveyor (Mining) T&S Gr. B. Category-wise figures for the Surveyor post should be verified against the official notification due to a possible formatting inconsistency in the source table.

faq.question: Q2. What is the last date to apply for NCL Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date for online submission of application is 05-08-2026, up to 11:59 PM. This is also the Crucial Date for determining eligibility and reservation/relaxation benefits.

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for NCL Mining Sirdar and Surveyor posts?
faq.answer: Candidates must be at least 18 years old and not more than 30 years old as on the Crucial Date (05-08-2026), with age relaxation available for SC/ST (5 years), OBC-NCL (3 years), Ex-Servicemen (service period + 3 years), and no upper age limit for Departmental Candidates.

faq.question: Q4. What is the application fee for NCL Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: General/OBC-NCL/EWS candidates must pay ₹1180/- (₹1000 + GST ₹180). SC/ST/ESM/Departmental candidates are exempted from the fee.

faq.question: Q5. What is the pay scale for Mining Sirdar and Surveyor posts?
faq.answer: Mining Sirdar T&S Gr. C carries a basic pay of ₹47,330.25 per month, and Surveyor T&S Gr. B carries a basic pay of ₹51,103.12 per month, plus other allowances as per the National Coal Wage Agreement.

faq.question: Q6. What is the selection process for NCL Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Selection is through a Computer Based Test (CBT) of 100 marks (90 minutes), followed by document scrutiny/verification for candidates who clear the cut-off marks, in order of merit.

faq.question: Q7. Can a candidate apply for both Mining Sirdar and Surveyor posts?
faq.answer: No, a candidate can apply for only a single post against this Employment Notification.

faq.question: Q8. What documents are needed for Mining Sirdar eligibility?
faq.answer: Candidates need a Matriculation certificate, a valid Mining Sirdar/Overman's Certificate of Competency issued by DGMS, a valid Gas Testing Certificate, and a valid First Aid Certificate (or a Mining Engineering degree/diploma with Overman certification as an alternate route).

faq.question: Q9. When will the Computer Based Test (CBT) be held?
faq.answer: The CBT date is not yet released in the notification. It will be intimated through a notice and the Admit Card, available for download through the individual login portal on the NCL website.

faq.question: Q10. Is there a probation period for these posts?
faq.answer: Yes, both Mining Sirdar and Surveyor posts carry a probation period of 6 months.

faq.question: How many vacancies are there in NCL Recruitment 2026 for Mining Sirdar and Surveyor?
faq.answer: As per the source notification, there are 254 vacancies for Mining Sirdar T&S Gr. C and 5 vacancies for Surveyor (Mining) T&S Gr. B. Category-wise figures for the Surveyor post should be verified against the official notification due to a possible formatting inconsistency in the source table.
